mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO rose from Feb 2009 to April 2009
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive monthly INDPRO changes are not positive in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor income and collapsing consumer demand, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE increased from 7.8% in Jan 2009 to 8.9% in Apr 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp over 3 months during any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL and a level of UNRATE above 7% indicate stagflationary pressure that compresses corporate margins and elevates equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9% and CPIAUCSL rising through early 2009
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not negative in more than 55% of joint trigger instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over main effects in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, monetary policy is constrained and retail sales growth becomes the primary driver of equity returns; month-over-month increases in RSAFS signal direct consumer spending recovery and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS changes fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly GDPC1 growth does not coincide with positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decreases in the federal funds rate inject immediate liquidity into funding markets, reducing short-term discount rates and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Decreases in FEDFUNDS fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of months over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in industrial production exceeding 2% signals a severe contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 2% over 3 months do not coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in housing starts exceeding 10% signals a rebound in residential construction and associated durable goods demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 510K in March 2009, up from prior lows
failure_condition: HOUST increases exceeding 10% over 3 months do not produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.
